mnemonics.title: Tree Properties
mnemonics.content: TREE: T-wo leaves minimum, R-ooted or free, E-very pair connected, E-dges = V-ertices -1, N-o cycles allowed

mnemonics.title: Tree Traversals
mnemonics.content: Pre-order: Root Left Right | In-order: Left Root Right | Post-order: Left Right Root | Think: When is Root visited?

mnemonics.title: Finding Tree Center
mnemonics.content: LEAF REMOVAL: List all leaves, Remove them, Repeat until 1-2 vertices remain - those are the center(s)

acronyms.usage: A binary tree with ordering property: left child < parent < right child, enabling efficient search.
Spanning tree with minimum total edge weight, found using Prim's or Kruskal's algorithms.
Tree containing shortest paths from source to all vertices, built by Dijkstra's algorithm.
Directed graph with no cycles, used in task scheduling, dependency resolution.

visualTips.visualization: Draw a single connected branch (tree) vs multiple disconnected branches (forest). Think: Forest = Forest of trees, disconnected.
Draw degree 1 vertices as 'end points' with single connection. Like tree leaves at tips of branches.
Take original graph, keep all vertices, remove edges one by one until no cycles remain. Visual: Pruning branches from a tree.
Imagine trimming outer edges (leaves) repeatedly. What's left in the middle after trimming is the center.
Inverted tree with root at top, children below, like organizational chart or family tree.

analogies.analogy: Like a family genealogy: root = ancestor, branches = descendants, leaves = last descendants with no children.
Like a subway map: keeps all stations (vertices) but removes unnecessary routes to avoid loops.
Like a binary decision: each step either go left or right, narrowing down possibilities.
Like reading a book: Pre-order = title then chapters, In-order = chapters in order, Post-order = references last.
